subject: Your Little Movers Tip for February - Tummy Time Wedge Trick
body: Hi Jessica! This month's tip: roll a small towel under your baby's chest during tummy time to reduce strain and extend tolerance. Try 3x daily for 5 minutes.

subject: Your Little Movers Tip for February - Tummy Time Wedge Trick
body: Hi Tanya! This month's tip: roll a small towel under your baby's chest during tummy time to reduce strain. Short, frequent sessions are better than one long one.

subject: Your Little Movers Tip for February - Tummy Time Wedge Trick
body: Hi Ben! February tip from Greg: a rolled towel under your child's chest makes tummy time more comfortable and builds tolerance faster. Try it after a diaper change.

subject: Your Little Movers Tip for March - Sitting Balance with a Rolled Blanket
body: Hi Priya! March tip: use a tightly rolled blanket behind your child's back as a support ring to practice unsupported sitting. Gradually reduce support as balance improves.

subject: Your Little Movers Tip for March - Making Crawling Fun
body: Hi Karyn! March tip: place a favorite toy just out of reach during crawling practice. Use a yoga mat for grip and comfort. Tunnel play encourages cross-body coordination.

subject: Your Little Movers Tip for March - Making Crawling Fun
body: Hi Nina! March tip from Greg: create a soft obstacle course with pillows and cushions to encourage crawling transitions. Keep sessions under 10 minutes and follow your child's lead.

subject: Your Little Movers Tip for March - Supported Standing at the Couch
body: Hi Lena! March tip: encourage pulling to stand using the couch cushion as a surface. Place a motivating toy on the cushion seat. Practice 5-8 pulls per session with rest breaks.

subject: Chapter 1 Is Live: Tummy Time Foundations - Little Movers
body: Hi Danielle! Great news - Chapter 1 of the Little Movers Guide is now live. Log in at littlemoversguide.com to access Tummy Time Foundations and the printable exercise tracker.

subject: Chapter 1 Is Live: Tummy Time Foundations - Little Movers
body: Hi Melissa! Chapter 1 of Little Movers is now live! Head to littlemoversguide.com to get started with Tummy Time Foundations and the 4-week exercise schedule for young infants.

subject: Your Little Movers Tip for March - Floor Play Positions
body: Hi Stephanie! March tip: vary floor play positions daily - side-lying, prone, and supine each build different muscle groups. Rotate every 15 minutes to keep things fresh and challenging.

subject: Chapter 2 Is Live: Building Sitting Balance - Little Movers
body: Hi Lindsey! Chapter 2 is here. Building Sitting Balance covers trunk strengthening and stability exercises you can do using household items. Login at littlemoversguide.com.

subject: Your Little Movers Tip for October - Hand-Knee Rocking
body: Hi Priya! October tip: practice hand-knee rocking before crawling. Place your child on all fours and gently rock forward and back. This builds the rhythm and confidence needed for full crawling.

subject: Chapter 3 Is Live: Crawling and Developmental Transitions
body: Hi Tanya! Chapter 3 of Little Movers is now live. Crawling and Developmental Transitions covers commando crawling, reciprocal crawling, and transitioning to standing. Visit littlemoversguide.com.

subject: Your Little Movers Tip for November - Cruising Along Furniture
body: Hi Karyn! November tip: encourage side-stepping along the couch or coffee table. Place toys at intervals to motivate movement. This builds the hip and ankle strength needed for walking.

subject: Chapter 4 Is Live: Getting Ready to Stand - Little Movers
body: Hi Nina! Chapter 4 is live! Getting Ready to Stand walks you through squat-to-stand practice, supported standing exercises, and how to set up a safe environment for cruising.

subject: New Year, New Milestones - Setting Goals With Little Movers
body: Hi Sarah! Happy New Year. For your patients' families, encourage three-month milestone goals with weekly check-ins. The Little Movers goal tracker worksheet is now available in the clinician portal.

subject: Your Little Movers Tip for February - Deep Pressure Warm-Up
body: Hi Danielle! February tip: begin each exercise session with 30 seconds of deep pressure along your child's arms and legs. This prepares the sensory system for motor activity and reduces resistance.

subject: Your Little Movers Tip for March - Stair Climbing Basics
body: Hi Gina! March tip: supervised stair climbing builds hip flexor strength and body awareness. Start with two stairs, alternate feet with hand support. Celebrate every step loudly - motivation matters!

subject: The Complete Little Movers Guide Is Here!
body: Hi Tamara! The full Little Movers Guide is now live at littlemoversguide.com - all four chapters, printable worksheets, and the video library. Your early access is active. Enjoy!

subject: Your Little Movers Tip for March - Pool Noodle Balance Bar
body: Hi James! March tip: hold a pool noodle horizontally at waist height as a balance bar while your child practices standing and early stepping. It's the perfect low-cost therapy tool for home use.

subject: Your Little Movers Tip for March - Barefoot Play on Varied Surfaces
body: Hi Danielle! March tip: barefoot play on grass, carpet, and smooth floors builds proprioceptive awareness critical for balance and walking. Try 10 minutes of supervised barefoot outdoor time daily.

subject: Your Little Movers Tip for March - Barefoot Play on Varied Surfaces
body: Hi Sarah! March tip for your families: barefoot play on varied textures improves proprioceptive input. Share this with parents whose children are working on standing balance and early ambulation.
